title.ro: Simplificator de text pentru Ã®nvÄƒÈ›are accesibilÄƒ

content.en:
Rewrite the following text to make it accessible for students with dyslexia or reading difficulties, at approximately a [target reading level, e.g. Grade 3] reading level:

[PASTE YOUR ORIGINAL TEXT HERE]

Apply these adaptations:
- Shorten sentences to a maximum of 15 words each
- Replace low-frequency vocabulary with simpler synonyms (keep subject-specific terms but define them)
- Use active voice throughout
- Break up long paragraphs into 2â€“3 sentence chunks
- Add a bold key term glossary at the end (max 5 terms)
- Suggest 2 places where a visual (diagram, image) would support comprehension

Do not change the factual content or meaning.

content.en:
Create three versions of the following assignment on [topic], differentiated by readiness level for [grade level] students:

Original task: [DESCRIBE THE ORIGINAL TASK]

Provide:
- Tier 1 (Support / Below Level): Heavily scaffolded version with sentence starters, word banks, a simplified version of the task, and visual supports
- Tier 2 (Core / On Level): The standard task with minor scaffolding hints and guiding questions
- Tier 3 (Extension / Above Level): An enriched version that adds complexity, requires higher-order thinking, or connects to a real-world application

For each tier, note: the key modification made and why it supports that learner group. All three tiers must assess the same core learning objective.

- Nivelul 2 (BazÄƒ / La nivel): Sarcina standard cu indicii de sprijin minore È™i Ã®ntrebÄƒri ghid
- Nivelul 3 (Extindere / Deasupra nivelului): O versiune Ã®mbogÄƒÈ›itÄƒ care adaugÄƒ complexitate, necesitÄƒ gÃ¢ndire de ordin superior sau conecteazÄƒ la o aplicare din lumea realÄƒ

content.en:
Generate a practical scaffolding support plan for English Language Learners (ELL) / multilingual learners studying [topic] in a [grade level] class. The students are at [language proficiency level, e.g. beginner / intermediate / advanced].

Include:
1. Pre-teaching vocabulary: 8â€“10 key terms with student-friendly definitions and a home-language connection tip
2. Visual scaffolds: 3 specific types of visual aids appropriate for this topic
3. Modified instructions: how to rewrite or chunk the main task instructions for clarity
4. Sentence frames: 5 useful sentence starters for discussion or written responses
5. Peer support strategy: one structured partner or small-group activity that builds both content knowledge and language skills
6. Assessment adaptation: how to assess content understanding without penalising language proficiency

2. Suporturi vizuale: 3 tipuri specifice de materiale vizuale potrivite pentru acest subiect
3. InstrucÈ›iuni modificate: cum sÄƒ rescrii sau sÄƒ segmentezi instrucÈ›iunile sarcinii principale pentru claritate
4. Structuri de propoziÈ›ii: 5 exemple de Ã®nceputuri de propoziÈ›ii utile pentru discuÈ›ii sau rÄƒspunsuri scrise
5. Strategie de suport de la colegi: o activitate structuratÄƒ Ã®n perechi sau grup mic care dezvoltÄƒ atÃ¢t cunoaÈ™terea conÈ›inutului, cÃ¢t È™i abilitÄƒÈ›ile lingvistice
6. Adaptare a evaluÄƒrii: cum sÄƒ evaluezi Ã®nÈ›elegerea conÈ›inutului fÄƒrÄƒ a penaliza competenÈ›a lingvisticÄƒ
